australia.name: Australia
australia.metaTitle: Australia migration hub | visas & pathways | MinRosh Migration
australia.metaDescription: Overview of Australian visa pathways with links to official Department of Home Affairs listings. Brisbane-based guidance for skilled, student, family, and employer-sponsored routes.
australia.headline: Australia migration hub
australia.intro: Australia’s visa system covers skilled points-tested visas, employer sponsorship, study routes, partner and family visas, and visitor options. Rules change with government policy, so always confirm current criteria on the Department of Home Affairs website before making decisions.

officialLinks.label: Department of Home Affairs — visa listing

sections.title: Skilled and points-tested pathways
sections.body: Skilled migration usually starts with occupation fit, skills assessment, English evidence, and an Expression of Interest where applicable. Subclass 189, 190, and 491 each have different nomination and residence obligations. Competitiveness depends on points, occupation ceilings, and invitation rounds—not only the legal minimum threshold.
bullets: Plan skills assessment and English timing before committing to a lodgement sequence
Compare independent, state-nominated, and regional options for your occupation
Use official visa pages to confirm eligibility, charges, and document requirements

sections.title: Study, graduate, and longer-term planning
sections.body: Student visas are temporary. Successful long-term planning often links course choice, genuine student requirements, and any post-study work or skilled pathways you may pursue later. Education choices should align with visa conditions and your realistic timeline.
bullets: Clarify enrolment, funds, and health insurance requirements early
Understand how study fits with any later skilled or sponsored pathway
Keep evidence consistent across study and migration applications

sections.title: Partner, family, and employer-sponsored routes
sections.body: Partner and family visas focus heavily on relationship evidence, sponsorship, and health and character requirements. Employer-sponsored routes depend on occupation lists, employer nomination, and your work history. Each stream has distinct processing and compliance expectations.
bullets: Partner pathways need coherent relationship evidence across categories
Sponsored work routes require alignment between employer, role, and visa criteria
Visitor visas need a clear lawful purpose and ties to home country

new-zealand.name: New Zealand
new-zealand.metaTitle: New Zealand migration hub | visa planning | MinRosh Migration
new-zealand.metaDescription: New Zealand visa planning with links to Immigration New Zealand’s find-a-visa tool. General guidance on study, work, and residence pathways—not legal advice.
new-zealand.headline: New Zealand migration hub
new-zealand.intro: New Zealand’s immigration system is structured around specific visa categories, each with its own criteria and application process. Use Immigration New Zealand’s official tools to identify which visa types might match your situation, then seek tailored advice for your circumstances.

officialLinks.label: Immigration New Zealand — find a visa

sections.title: How to approach NZ visa selection
sections.body: Start by clarifying your immediate goal—short visit, study, skilled work, partnership, or residence—and your timeline. The find-a-visa tool helps narrow categories; from there, read the official instructions for the visa you are considering.
bullets: Confirm passport, health, and character requirements for your category
Gather employment or study offers where the visa depends on them
Track policy updates that affect quotas or skill lists

sections.title: Study and work transitions
sections.body: Many applicants combine study with later work rights. Planning should account for course approval, funds, and any post-study work conditions that apply to the visa you hold.
bullets: Align institution and course with visa conditions
Plan evidence for genuine intent and funds
Understand how work rights differ by visa type

canada.name: Canada
canada.metaTitle: Canada migration hub | IRCC entry & visa planning | MinRosh Migration
canada.metaDescription: Canada immigration and visit planning with reference to Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada. General guidance on entry requirements and visa categories.
canada.headline: Canada migration hub
canada.intro: Canada’s immigration services are delivered through IRCC, with distinct programs for visitors, students, workers, and permanent residence. Entry rules can depend on your nationality, purpose of travel, and documents such as an eTA or visa.

sections.title: Visitors, study, and work permits
sections.body: Temporary residence categories each have specific eligibility, documents, and processing considerations. Permanent residence programs (such as Express Entry or provincial nominations) sit on separate tracks with their own points or selection rules.
bullets: Check whether you need a visitor visa or eTA for short stays
Study permits require a designated learning institution acceptance in most cases
Work permits often need employer involvement or a Labour Market Impact Assessment

sections.title: Using official sources
sections.body: IRCC publishes the authoritative eligibility tools and document checklists. Use them to validate assumptions before you invest in language tests or educational credential assessments.
bullets: Use IRCC’s own questionnaires for visit and transit needs
Track processing time notices and form editions
Keep biometrics and medical exam requirements in mind

united-kingdom.name: United Kingdom
united-kingdom.metaTitle: United Kingdom migration hub | visas & immigration | MinRosh Migration
united-kingdom.metaDescription: UK visa and immigration planning with links to GOV.UK. General information on work, study, and family routes—not legal advice.
united-kingdom.headline: United Kingdom migration hub
united-kingdom.intro: The UK visa system includes visitor, study, work, and family routes, each governed by the Immigration Rules. GOV.UK is the primary place to confirm which visa matches your situation and what evidence you will need.

sections.title: Work and study routes
sections.body: Skilled Worker and Student visas are common sponsored categories. Eligibility typically involves a licensed sponsor, English language, maintenance funds, and sometimes a Certificate of Sponsorship or Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies.
bullets: Confirm sponsor licence status and role eligibility
Meet English and financial thresholds for your route
Understand visa length and conditions before you travel

sections.title: Family and visitors
sections.body: Family routes depend on the relationship and sometimes income thresholds. Standard Visitor visas require a credible travel purpose and intention to leave the UK after the visit.
bullets: Gather relationship and financial evidence for family applications
Visitor applications should explain ties to your home country
Check tuberculosis testing requirements where they apply
